headline: Canada is mid-build on EV infrastructure: deployment is funded, policy is set, but execution capacity is the bottleneck.

metrics.label: Federal ZEV mandate (light-duty new sales)
metrics.context: Backed by federal Electric Vehicle Availability Standard.

metrics.label: Public charging ports installed
metrics.context: Mix of Level 2 and DC fast; concentrated in ON, QC, BC.

metrics.label: Public ports estimated by 2035
metrics.context: Order-of-magnitude planning estimate to support 100% ZEV new sales.

metrics.label: ZEVIP federal funding committed
metrics.context: Cost-share for public, workplace, multi-unit, and fleet charging.

metrics.label: Fleet share of light + medium duty VKT
metrics.context: High utilization; primary near-term ROI for depot charging + BESS.

metrics.label: Home charging share of EV energy
metrics.context: Indicates residential install + smart panel as recurring channel.

metrics.label: Tariff applied to Chinese-made EVs (Canada)
metrics.context: Primary reason to lead with infrastructure, software, and BESS rather than vehicles.

metrics.label: Typical DCFC grid interconnect lead time
metrics.context: Largest single execution risk; argues for BESS-buffered sites.
